title: Simple animal from circles
summary: Easy lesson for drawing a neutral animal from basic shapes.
steps: Draw a large circle for the head.
Add two triangles for ears.
Add eyes, nose and whiskers.
warnings: Use an original neutral topic, not a known character.

title: Simple animal — full lesson
summary: Practice card for an animal built from basic shapes.
steps: Draw the head circle and smaller muzzle circle.
Add triangle ears.
Draw dot eyes and a small nose.
Build the body from an oval.
Add four short oval paws.
Finish with tail, whiskers and soft shadows.
warnings: This practices shapes, not copying characters.
dontDo: do not use names of known characters

title: Simple car from rectangles
summary: Short vehicle lesson from simple shapes.
steps: Draw a long rectangle for the body.
Add a smaller rectangle for the cabin.
Draw two wheels.
warnings: Do not copy a specific branded model.

title: Vehicle — full lesson
summary: Practice card for a generic vehicle without logos.
steps: Draw a ground line.
Add a rectangle body.
Add a cabin with a rounded roof.
Draw two wheels with centers.
Add windows, bumper and lights.
Add shadow and a simple road.
dontDo: do not copy a real car exactly

title: Plant in a pot
summary: Simple plant lesson with leaves and a pot.
steps: Draw a trapezoid pot.
Add a stem.
Add several oval leaves.
warnings: Leaves may be uneven; that is part of practice.
dontDo: do not endlessly fix every leaf

title: Plant — full lesson
summary: Practice repeated shapes through a simple plant.
steps: Start with a trapezoid pot.
Add a narrow rim rectangle.
Draw three stems of different height.
Add oval leaves.
Draw thin lines inside leaves.
Finish with soil, shadow and simple color.
dontDo: do not press too hard during sketching

title: Simple figure from shapes
summary: Neutral lesson for drawing a simple person without copying a known character.
steps: Draw a circle for the head.
Add a rectangle body.
Draw arms and legs with simple lines.
warnings: Do not copy characters from cartoons, games or movies.
dontDo: do not use known character names

title: Simple figure — full lesson
summary: Practice card for a neutral person made from shapes.
steps: Start with a head circle.
Add neck and rectangle body.
Draw arms as simple tubes.
Build legs from two longer rectangles.
Add simple shoes and hands.
Add a neutral face and original clothing.
warnings: The figure should be original and neutral.

title: Box robot
summary: Simple robot from squares, rectangles and circles.
steps: Draw a square head.
Add a rectangle body.
Draw arms, legs and antenna.
warnings: The robot should be your own design.

title: Robot — full lesson
summary: Practice card for a geometric robot.
steps: Draw a square head.
Add neck and rectangle body.
Build arms from two segments on each side.
Make legs from rectangles and small feet.
Add eyes, button panel and antenna.
Finish with your own pattern on the robot belly.
warnings: Do not use the look of known robots from movies or games.

title: Small house
summary: Short house lesson from simple shapes.
steps: Draw a square wall.
Add a triangle roof.
Draw a door and two windows.
warnings: Do not worry about perfect symmetry.
dontDo: do not press the sketch too hard

title: House — full lesson
summary: Simple house practice card with background.
steps: Draw a square or rectangle wall.
Add a triangle roof.
Draw door, handle and two windows.
Add chimney and simple fence.
Draw a path with two widening lines.
Finish with grass, cloud and house shadow.

title: Original small monster
summary: Create a funny neutral monster from your own shapes.
steps: Draw any oval as the body.
Add one, two or three eyes.
Draw short legs and a funny smile.
warnings: The monster should be original, not from a film or game.

title: Monster — full lesson
summary: Practice card for an original neutral monster character.
steps: Start with an unusual oval or cloud shape as body.
Add eyes in different sizes.
Draw mouth, teeth or tongue in simple style.
Add arms as short lines or paws.
Add belly pattern: dots, stripes or patches.
Create an original descriptive name without brand references.
dontDo: do not use protected character names

title: Simple drawing lesson
summary: Universal start for a neutral step-by-step drawing.
steps: Start from one large simple shape.
Add smaller elements.
Correct only the most important lines at the end.
warnings: Do not copy protected characters or logos.

title: Universal practice card
summary: Method for building any neutral drawing.
steps: Choose a topic without brands or known characters.
Break it into 2–3 simple shapes.
Sketch the largest shape lightly.
Add smaller elements and details.
Improve the outline only after the sketch.
Finish with shadow, background or color.
